rulings.Case Title: EDITIONS LIMITED WEST, INC. VS. SOMERSET STUDIOS INCORPORATED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 7, 2015, Line 1, PETITIONER EDITIONS LIMITED WEST, INC.'S Motion To Tax Costs. Granted. No timely opposition filed. Respondent did not file his opposition brief until June 30, 2015, one week after it was due. (See CCP sec. 1005(b).) Additionally, the proof of service indicates that the document was served by mail from North Carolina on June 30, 2015. CCP sec. 1005(c) provides that all opposing papers shall be served in a manner "reasonably calculated to ensure delivery to the other party or parties not later than the close of the next business day." Respondent did not serve the opposition in compliance with this statute. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: FARHAD ZAGHI VS. CECELIA KENNEDY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Authorizing The Taking Of Deposition Of Incarcerated Cross-Defendant Scott Kinney Aka Karim Akil
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, July 7, 2015, Line 1, CROSS COMPLAINANT STEWART TITLE OF CALIFORNIA, INC.'s Motion For Order Authorizing The Taking Of Deposition Of Incarcerated Cross-Defendant Scott Kinney Aka Karim Akil. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Cross defendant's uncontested motion for permission to depose incarcerated cross-defendant, Scott Kinney, is granted. The proposed Order attached to the moving papers will be signed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JILMA COREA VS. SONIA COREA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order To Compel Answers To Interrogatories And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, July 7, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANT SONIA COREA's Motion For Order To Compel Answers To Interrogatories And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel answers to interrogatories and for sanctions is granted. Service by plaintiff of the non-verified response was of no force and effect. The plaintiff shall serve a code complaint response to defendant's Supplemental Interrogatory number 1 within 10 court days after service on plaintiff's counsel of this Order with Notice of Entry. Interrogatories titled Supplemental Interrogatory numbers 2 and 3 are Special Interrogatories. The plaintiff shall serve a code complaint responses to same within the aforesaid ten court days. Sanctions are awarded against plaintiff's counsel only in the sum of $750.00 plus $98.05 in motion costs, to be paid simultaneously with service of the code compliant discovery responses. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DENNIS MARS VS. LAW OFFICE OF LAUREL MOUSSEAU AN UNKNOWN ENTITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 7, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANTS LAW OFFICE OF LAUREL MOUSSEAU AN UNKNOWN ENTITY, AND LAUREL MOUSSEAU'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled. The complaint adequately alleges the elements of professional negligence. Additionally, if there are any sufficient allegations to entitle plaintiff to relief, other allegations cannot be challenged by general demurrer. (Kong v. City of Hawaiian Garden Redevelop. Agency (2003) 108 Cal.App.4th 1028, 1046.) Liberally construing the complaint, the allegations do not establish that all of Plaintiff's potential causes of action were barred by the statute of limitations at the time that Plaintiff hired Defendant. For example, Plaintiff alleges that he wanted Defendant to bring a breach of contract cause of action against the union. (Complaint, par. 8.) The statute of limitations for breach of a written contract is four years. The complaint alleges that Plaintiff hired Defendant within four years of his termination (Complaint, pars. 8 and 14.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
